If one is not in a position to reform it, he should try to acquire the help of those people who can reform it or catch his hand. This reality was explained by the Holy Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) in the parable of the passengers of a ship.
If any person comes to know of an evil that is limited only to his own person, he should advise him if he is in a position to advise. If he is not in that position, he should lower his gaze (ghadd-i-basar) from it and cover it up. Allah Almighty covers up the faults of those who cover up the faults of others. But if the evil is of a contagious nature, then he should act upon that instruction of the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) which has been described in the Hadith of forbidding evil (Inkar-i-Munkar).
The Method of Reflecting on the Quran and Hadith 
In understanding the Quran and Hadith, people face confusion mostly because they do not employ reflection in fixing the context and placement of verses and traditions. There is no doubt that in some traditions, the virtue of covering up the sins of one's Muslim brother has been described. But if any gentleman, merely on the basis of this Hadith, gives this fatwa: 'We have nothing to do with the virtue or vice of others, we have to relate only to our own person; even if any vice comes to our knowledge from someone, we should cover it up, Allah Almighty will cover up our sins on the Day of Resurrection,' then although a Hadith is present in support of this fatwa, this fatwa will create a great entanglement in minds, because in other traditions, this matter has also been described with extreme clarity: 'If any of you sees an evil, he should reform it with his hand if he possesses the strength for it; if he does not possess the strength, he should reform it with his tongue; if he does not possess the strength for that either, he should consider it bad in his heart, and there is no stage of faith below this.'
This entanglement will obviously arise because an effort was not made to fix the context and placement of both traditions. If the context and placement of both are fixed, no entanglement will arise. If you see a person standing and urinating, you can lower your gaze thinking that it is possible there is an excuse. If the excuse is not clear, you can advise him in an excellent manner that this method is against civility, refinement, and Islamic etiquettes of cleanliness. If you are not in a position to advise, cover up this crudeness of his, do not publicize it; inshallah, this covering up of yours will be a cause of reward for you in the sight of Allah. But if regarding a person you possess the knowledge that he has set up a wine distillery in his house, or has gathered a store of hashish, or has hidden weapons, or has established a brothel, and you, despite being in a position to inform the police and government, are covering it up with the thought that on the Day of Resurrection Allah Almighty will cover up your sins, then in my view, this is merely an optimistic delusion. People fallen into this kind of optimistic delusion, far from earning reward, will lose their faith itself.
Misunderstanding of Some People in the Chapter of Backbiting 
In the chapter of backbiting as well, some people have created a similar type of confusion of issues due to a lack of reflection. While fixing the boundaries of backbiting, they completely forgot that just as the prohibition of backbiting has appeared in the Quran and Hadith, similarly, the commands of critique and validation (jarh wa ta'dil), true testimony, forbidding evil, and well-wishing for Muslims have also appeared in highly positive and positive words. When the question of reconciliation between the two arose and no reconciliation came to their understanding, they created a reconciliation in this manner: 'These matters do fall under backbiting, but they have been made permissible because strategy required them.' The commands of critique and validation, true testimony, forbidding evil, and advising Muslims are not among the permissible matters (mubahat) but among the duties (wajibat) of religion. The entire beauty and perfection of the Islamic system depend on them. On the investigation of narrators and critique and validation is the foundation of the science of Shariah. True testimony is that official duty of this Ummah for which it was raised in the world. With forbidding evil, Allah and the Messenger (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) have bound the survival and permanence of this Ummah. Similarly, the well-wishing of Muslims is not just a requirement of brotherhood; rather, you have already read in this Surah that it is a requirement of faith too. These things have been made obligatory upon the Ummah not by making backbiting permissible for them, but have been made obligatory under the requirements of faith. As for backbiting, it has a specific sphere, the explanation of which we have carried out under the verse. Its prohibition is not an intruder or blocker from any aspect in these duties of religion such that a need to make it permissible for the sake of strategy should arise. Neither did the Muhaddithin declare it allowed, nor did the revivers and reformers, nor can any Muslim ever face the need to make it permissible for the discharge of any of his religious duties. The need for it can arise only for those people who want to make the Islamic Shariah a playground for their strategy.

-6 The Subject of the Upcoming Verses 14-18 
Ahead are the concluding verses of the Surah, in which the inner self of those people has been unveiled whose attitude was reproached in the initial five verses. There we have already indicated that some tribes from the outskirts of Medina, seeing the rising power of Islam, had joined the Muslims, but due to being far from the center, their training had not been carried out well. For this reason, they were involved in the delusion that it is a huge favor from them upon the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) and Islam that they entered Islam without any fighting. It is obvious that when they sat assuming themselves to be the benefactors of Allah and His Messenger, how could they observe that respect and honor of the Messenger of Allah (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) which faith required? Consequently, such actions were issued from them upon which a grasp has been made in the initial verses, though the style of address remained general so that whoever possessed these flaws should reform them. Consequently, those things whose teaching this situation required were told. Now, in the end, along with the specification of their name, their original disease has been pointed out so that they can turn toward its treatment, because while this disease exists, the growth of faith is impossible. Please recite the verses in this light:
-7 Word Research and Explanation of Verses 
Qalatil-a'rabu amanna qul lam tu'minu wa lakin qulu aslamna wa lamma yadkhulil-imanu fi qulubikum wa in tuti'ullaha wa rasulahu la yalitkum min a'malikum shay'a innallaha ghafurur-rahim 14
Blow to the Pride of Those Who Considered Themselves Benefactors of Islam 
By 'a'rab' is meant those very rustic people from the outskirts of Medina who have been mentioned above: that when they speak to the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him), they address you in the manner a person addresses a man of equal status. If ever they come to meet you, their desire immediately upon arrival is that the Holy Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) should meet them without delay. To the extent that if you are present inside the house, they do not tolerate taking the trouble to wait; instead, they start calling out your name from outside the house. In this crudeness of theirs, where deprivation of training had a part, this matter also had a part: that these people were involved in the illusion that by entering Islam without any war and conflict, they have done a favor upon you (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him), the reward for which they should get is that the Holy Prophet should consider them as his and Islam's benefactors and indulge their whims at every opportunity. A blow is being struck here at this very mindset of these people.
'Qalatil-a'rabu amanna': It is stated that these Bedouins say, 'We have believed!' The reference to this statement of theirs here is not given merely as their declaration of faith, but it will become clear from the upcoming verses that they used to say this by way of displaying a favor. Meaning, they used to remind the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) at every turn of this favor that they enhanced your respect and power by accepting faith. For this reason, they are deserving that the Prophet should observe their regard everywhere and accept whatever advice they give with eyes and heart.
'Qul lam tu'minu wa lakin qulu aslamna': A reply has been given to them through the blessed tongue of the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him): tell them that this claim of yours is not correct that you have believed; however, you can make this claim that you have submitted. The word 'Islam' here is in its literal meaning, i.e., outward obedience. The true meaning of Islam is to hand over one's self entirely to one's Lord, but it also comes in the meaning of mere outward obedience. The explanation of both these meanings has passed in its place; here it has been used in this second meaning. Regarding these people, we have already shown above that rather than the invitation of faith, being overawed by the rising political power of Islam, these people had joined the Muslims. For this reason, the spirit of faith had not yet descended into their hearts, though they had come under the political values of Islam. Pointing to this very reality, he replied that the claim of faith does not befit you yet; however, you can say that you have bowed your head before the power of Islam. Within this reply, this matter is hidden: when you have obeyed by being overawed by the political power of Islam, then this thing is not for reminding as a favor; rather, this too is a kind of being vanquished, the only difference being that you were vanquished without putting up a fight, and this thing is not such that you should remind of it as a favor.
The Reality of Faith 
'Wa lamma yadkhulil-imanu fi qulubikum', meaning: do not advance the support of your faith too much yet; it has knocked at the door of your hearts necessarily, but it has not entered inside the hearts. This faith is not credible with Allah. The faith credible with Allah is that which descends into the veins and muscles and colors the heart in its color in such a manner that thinking apart from it and performing any action does not remain easy for man.
'Fa-in tuti'ullaha wa rasulahu la yalitkum min a'malikum shay'a': This is an admonition on their display of favor: if you believed or rendered some service to Islam, why do you remind of its favor! Allah is not one to reduce anything at all from your deed; rather, He is the one to give a full reward for every small and large deed. You will do one, you will get seventy. Your every deed is going to come to your own use, it is not going to come to God's use. So when you are performing your own work, why do you place its favor upon Allah and the Messenger!
'Innallaha ghafurur-rahim': Allah Almighty is highly Forgiving and Merciful. He will overlook your shortcomings and flaws, and will not make any reduction at all in giving the reward. There is no fear at all of this matter that He should make your small defects an excuse to reduce the value of your deeds.
In this, there is also this hint: if until now this error remained seated in your mind that whatever you did for Islam is your favor upon Allah and the Messenger, then now after this warning, you should reform this error and ask for forgiveness from Allah; He will forgive you. He is highly Forgiving and Merciful.

Innamal-mu'minunalladhina amanu billahi wa rasulihi thumma lam yartabu wa jahadu bi-amwalihim wa anfusihim fi sabilillahi ula'ika humus-sadiqun 15
Attributes of the True People of Faith 
It is stated that every claimant of faith cannot become a believer in the sight of Allah. The true believers in the sight of Allah are only those who believed in Allah and the Messenger with a sincere heart, then did not fall into doubt and hesitation, but rather regularly fought with both wealth and life in the way of Allah. They spent their wealth as well for the strengthening and support of the religion, and when the turn came to sacrifice life, they did not hesitate from that either. It is stated that these very people are true in their claim of faith. As for those people who make the claim of faith with great loudness but due to their hesitation are neither ready to take any blow in this path nor possess any courage for the sacrifice of life and wealth, they are merely displays of passion. In the sight of Allah Almighty, they do not possess any value.
If you reflect here, it will be known that the mention of 'wa jahadu bi-amwalihim wa anfusihim' has occurred as a testimony of their lack of hesitation. If a person does not hesitate from the sacrifice of life and wealth for an ideal, then this is an undeniable testimony to the fact that he possesses full certainty in the truth of that ideal; and if for its sake he is neither ready to sacrifice wealth nor possesses the courage to put his life in any danger, then although he may make any amount of boast in its love, his deed is witness that he is still involved in doubt regarding it.

Qul a-tu'allimunallaha bi-dinikum wallahu ya'lamu ma fis-samawati wa ma fil-ard wallahu bi-kulli shay'in 'alim 16
Meaning: These people say in a highly patronizing style that 'we have believed,' so ask them: are you informing Allah of your religion? If these people are informing Allah, then tell them that Allah knows everything in the heavens and everything in the earth, and Allah is aware of everything. He knows everything actively as well, and is aware of everything attributively too. No thing at all is hidden or covered from Him. The meaning is that if these people are proud of their faith, then let them be proud of it before someone who is unaware of their religion and faith. What is the benefit of being proud before Him who is well-aware of every secret and open matter of this universe? Will He who is aware of everything not be aware of the length and breadth of their faith?

Yamunnuna 'alayka an aslamu qul la tamunnu 'alayya islamakum balillahu yamunnu 'alaykum an hadakum lil-imani in kuntum sadiqin 17
Reply to the Claimants of Favor 
After exposing the claim of faith of these people, the reality of their claim of Islam has been made clear: these people remind you (the address is to the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him)) of a favor that they accepted Islam. Meaning, their assumption is that by accepting Islam, they enhanced the Prophet's respect and gave power and glory to Islam, for which reason they are the benefactors of both the Prophet and Islam, and it is the Prophet's duty to respect this favor of theirs. It is stated: tell these people, do not remind me of the favor of your Islam. If you are indeed true in your claim, then your favor is not upon me, but the grace and favor of Allah Almighty is upon you that He granted you the tawfiq for faith. Keep an eye on the words 'in kuntum sadiqin' (if you are truthful): meaning, first of all, your claim of faith and Islam is itself mere boast; and if there is some truth in your statement, then you should be grateful to Allah that He granted you the tawfiq for it. The particle 'li' after guidance (hidayat) is proof that this word contains the subject of tawfiq here. Its explanation has passed in its place.
From this, this reality became clear: by rendering any small or large service to the religion, a person does a favor neither upon Allah and the Messenger nor upon the religion; rather, he does a favor upon his own self that he refines his end. The favor, in reality, is Allah Almighty's upon him that He opens the path of eternal success for him by granting him the tawfiq to serve His religion. Here, let there be an eye on this matter too that a reply was not caused to be given from the tongue of the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) that 'you people do not remind me of the favor of your faith and Islam, but my favor is upon you that I opened the path of guidance before you.' If this reply had been caused to be given, it had a place, but what the Prophet can do is only this much that he spends all his power to bring people onto the path of guidance, but granting people the tawfiq for guidance is not in his choice. Granting its tawfiq is in the choice of Allah Almighty alone, and the entire reliance of the matter is on this very granting of tawfiq.

Innallaha ya'lamu ghaybas-samawati wal-ard wallahu basirum bima ta'malun 18
That same subject of Verse 16 has been described in another style: there is no need to describe and remind of your faith and Islam too much. Allah knows all the secrets of the heavens and the earth on His own, and remember this that Allah is watching all your deeds. The meaning is that the real thing is not the claim but the deed. If you perform deeds, your claim will be proven in the sight of Allah without display and declaration; and if you do not perform deeds, regardless of how much you claim with the tongue, it will be completely realityless and useless.
With the tawfiq of the Almighty, the commentary on the last Surah of this group concluded on these lines. So all praise is due to Allah for that.
